_comment: Chesterfield County FY2026 Adopted Budget. Figures verified against the official 442-page budget book (see source_url). General Fund view (p.88 / consolidated p.84) is used as the primary 'where local tax dollars go' picture, per the all-funds caveat. Percentages reconcile to the General Fund total of $1,043,555,400.
adopted: Adopted by the Board of Supervisors on April 9, 2025

revenue.name: Other local taxes
revenue.note: Local sales tax, business license (BPOL), lodging, utility, recordation

revenue.name: State & federal aid
revenue.note: Intergovernmental revenue from the Commonwealth and federal government

revenue.name: Service charges, fees & permits
revenue.note: Charges for services, use of money/property, permits and fees

revenue.name: Reserves & other
revenue.note: Use of reserves, transfers in, recovered costs, fines

tax_rates.name: Real estate (real property)
tax_rates.note: Calendar year 2025. Fourth straight annual cut (was $0.90).

tax_rates.name: Personal property (vehicles)
tax_rates.note: Down from $3.60. 2025 personal property tax relief: 40%.

tax_rates.name: Data center computer equipment
tax_rates.note: Reduced rate for data center hardware.

tax_rates.name: Lodging (transient occupancy)
tax_rates.note: 6% to tourism, 2% to the General Fund.

tax_rates.name: Meals / food & beverage
tax_rates.note: Chesterfield County does not levy a meals tax.

source_title: Chesterfield County FY2026 Adopted Budget and FY2026–FY2030 Capital Improvement Program
